characters.physical_details: Lean, sun-browned, dark curly hair, sleepless-looking face, old prop scar on left calf.

key_relationships.tomas_acosta: Lover and former source of illicit night-channel access.
key_relationships.imogen_price: Employer and later restricted-role rehiring authority.
key_relationships.jonah_pike: Former lover tied to the cooler incident.

characters.occupation_status: Former disgraced field guide; starts on a temporary 640-dollar-per-week day-guide and utility contract, is fired in chapter_16, and is rehired in chapter_18 as utility and restricted guide at lower pay.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English and Spanish; handles permits, field manuals, and biotech paperwork.

state_transitions.change: Publicly re-identified with the BrightCurrent scandal.
Recovers the Annex B log and her old visitor badge.
Publicly confesses the cooler incident and loses camp employment.
Returns to payroll in a restricted role and lives in the former linen closet.

characters.physical_details: Broad-shouldered, engine-greased, crooked nose, old prop nicks, cheap reading glasses for logs.

key_relationships.mina_cordero: Lover; gives her illicit county-channel access.

characters.occupation_status: County mosquito-control airboat pilot through the chapter_11 suspension; by chapter_18 he is off county payroll, doing repair labor around camp, and still receiving county hearing notices.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English, speaks functional Spanish, and handles fuel logs and manuals.

state_transitions.change: Starts falsifying fuel use and giving Mina illicit night access.
Suspended; loses fuel key, route tablet, gate fob, and overtime.
Returns as local support during the emergency spray.
Refuses specimen-taking on the final run.
Works around camp without county payroll.

characters.physical_details: Tall, narrow-faced, dark hair braided for fieldwork, usually carrying guano and alcohol smells.
characters.occupation_status: University-linked bat surveyor; by the coda her work is centered on contamination monitoring.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in English; comfortable with scientific and legal prose; conversational Spanish for field use.

state_transitions.change: Joins the affair and recognizes the BrightCurrent tunnel link.
Learns Mina's full cooler history.
Keeps the raw spoonbill-sighting coordinates off Imogen's desk by calling the metadata unstable.
Deletes the breeding-site coordinate mark and phone pin.

characters.physical_details: Sun-creased, silver hair pinned up, donor-ready field shirts, reading glasses hidden from guests.

key_relationships.mina_cordero: Employer who later rehires her in a restricted role.
key_relationships.tomas_acosta: Camp ally with county access, later repair worker.
key_relationships.rhea_acosta: Contract scientist tied to camp legitimacy.

characters.occupation_status: Director of Rookery Key Bird Camp, balancing staffing, donors, permits, and repairs before and after the spray damage.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in English and fluent in grant, donor, and compliance paperwork.

state_transitions.change: Keeps Mina only through spoonbill week unless her utility exceeds her damage.
Treats the eel as a possible camp lifeline.
Loses narrative control and fires Mina publicly.
Rehires Mina on paper in a restricted lower-pay role.

characters.physical_details: Skinny, quick, oversized clothes, visible kitchen-burn scar on one wrist.
characters.occupation_status: Camp utility worker, kitchen helper, and repair assistant saving for trade school; by the coda he is studying marine systems and scores 91 percent on a practice section.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English, speaks English and home-register Spanish, and studies diesel and marine-systems workbooks.

state_transitions.change: Passes Mina the blue-eel rumor.
His trade-school ambitions become explicit.
Helps collect fallen chicks and dead fish after the spray.

characters.physical_details: Offstage older guide known through Mina's memory and the confession scene.

key_relationships.mina_cordero: Former lover and senior guide who had her carry the cooler.
key_relationships.imogen_price: Old professional contact from the guide circuit.

characters.occupation_status: Former senior field guide and BrightCurrent-affiliated interpreter; physically absent in the present timeline.
characters.literacy_languages: Functionally literate in English; writes persuasive guide copy and reads permits selectively.

state_transitions.change: Is named outright as the person behind the cooler incident.

timeline.story_start: Late March 2028, dry season, southwest Florida mangrove coast, with spoonbills pairing and water low enough to expose buried infrastructure.
timeline.estimated_span: About ten to twelve weeks from late March through May and into the first habitual rains; chapter_18 is weeks later, not months later.

seasonal_track.season: Late dry season; low water, rising heat, donors, spoonbill pairing.
Breeding peak with storm fronts and tightening financial pressure.
Pre-rain crisis: annex discovery, spray damage, confession, final no-engine run.

key_events.event: Mina arrives at Rookery Key on a temporary contract worth 640 dollars per week plus a bed.
Mina chooses illegal night access through Tomas after legal routes fail.
First illegal run produces a blue sighting under the spoonbills.
Rhea and Mina find the BrightCurrent intake route under camp and begin their affair.
Donor weekend publicly reconnects Mina to the BrightCurrent scandal.
Mina tells Tomas and Rhea about Jonah Pike and the white cooler.
All three witness the eel clearly, but the recording remains partial.
A county audit suspends Tomas and strips his night-route access.
Mina finds the Annex B log proving storm-loss release through the east sluice.
Emergency mosquito spraying damages the rookery during a public-health scare.
Mina publicly confesses and turns over the logbook and badge; guest channel access is frozen.
The final no-engine run reveals breeding adults and luminous egg clusters in a hidden mangrove basin.
Weeks later Mina is back on payroll in a reduced role while the camp works under monitoring restrictions.

geography.primary_setting: Rookery Key Bird Camp on the southwest Florida mangrove coast: a stilt-built field station above blackwater channels, spoonbill habitat, and buried BrightCurrent intake infrastructure.

key_locations.name: Rookery Key main house
key_locations.details: Stilted camp building with guest rooms, kitchen, screened walks, leaks, and Mina's later linen-closet room.

key_locations.name: Bat lab and survey room
key_locations.details: Small work building with nets, acoustic gear, batteries, and Rhea's data station.

key_locations.name: County mosquito dock
key_locations.details: Fuel cage, route logs, district office, and launch point for night access and spray support.

key_locations.name: Spoonbill rookery channels
key_locations.details: Protected blackwater creeks with loud colonies, shallow roots, insects, and the first blue sightings.

key_locations.name: BrightCurrent intake route under camp
key_locations.details: Low-tide concrete access beneath mangroves; entrance signage is degraded, while recovered records tie the wet system to Annex B.

key_locations.name: West mangrove root basin
key_locations.details: Hidden basin reached on the final skiff run; contains adult eels, luminous egg clusters, and no public coordinates.

key_locations.name: Observation platform and north rookery walk
key_locations.details: Guest-facing deck and access walk later marked as post-spray monitoring area with staff-escort limits.

distances: The main house and bat lab are linked by a short screened walk, close enough for voices to carry.
The county mosquito dock is about a ten-minute walk from camp.
The nearest rookery channels are reachable in under twenty minutes by airboat but much slower by paddle or pole.
The intake route is reachable on foot only at low tide through mud and roots.
The west mangrove basin is accessible only by stripped skiff and push pole.

world_rules: Night entry into the protected channels requires county authorization, a research permit filed about thirty days in advance, or an unencumbered wildlife credential.
BrightCurrent's luminescent eel line was built as sentinel stock, so release, concealment, or specimen-taking carries liability.
County mosquito-control infrastructure controls the easiest fuel, boat, and emergency-spray access to the creeks.
Storm timing and tide height are hard constraints; the annex route is only safely reachable in low-tide windows.
Chain-of-custody matters; weak files, borrowed gear, and corrupted metadata do not become clean proof.
The camp runs under chronic material scarcity, so jobs, housing, donor optics, and ecological decisions are entangled.

power_structure.mechanism: Payroll, beds, schedules, guest-facing roles, and camp messaging.
Payroll, route assignment, fuel key control, suspension power, and emergency spray authority.
Survey funding, permit legitimacy, data expectations, and indirect housing leverage.

power_structure.holder: Donors, board members, and camp creditors
power_structure.mechanism: Operating cash, repair survival, and publicity pressure.

power_structure.holder: Gulf Wildlife Board investigators and liability teams
power_structure.over: Everyone tied to the eel evidence
power_structure.mechanism: Evidence custody, access freezes, mortality review, and site restrictions.

objects.name: County fuel key
objects.owner: Tomas Acosta through county work access
objects.origin: Issued for mosquito-district fueling and route compliance.
objects.status: Used for Mina's illegal runs, then surrendered at suspension.

objects.name: White marine cooler
objects.origin: Used in the older BrightCurrent smuggling incident Mina confesses to.
objects.status: Historical object only; not recovered in the present timeline.

objects.name: Corrupted eel recording and duplicate drives
objects.origin: Partial recording made during the shared sighting.
objects.status: Copied to two local drives, kept off the cloud, and still inconclusive.

objects.name: County Pelican inspection camera and custody kit
objects.origin: County inspection gear pulled in for cleaner documentation.
objects.status: Part of the failed push to formalize the eel evidence.

objects.name: Annex B wet systems maintenance log
objects.owner: Taken by Mina, then bagged by investigators
objects.status: Documents east-sluice storm release, luminescent-line concealment, and at least forty-seven losses.

objects.name: Mina's BrightCurrent visitor-interpreter badge
objects.owner: Mina Cordero, then investigators as evidence
objects.origin: Her old BrightCurrent credential with Annex B escort access.
objects.status: Recovered from the annex and turned over with the logbook.

objects.name: Borrowed flat-bottomed skiff
objects.owner: Tomas's cousin, used by Mina, Rhea, and Tomas
objects.origin: Cheap fiberglass canal skiff borrowed for the final run.
objects.status: Stripped of net, cooler, baitwell, and engine bracket so the trip can stay witness-only.

debts.creditor: Gulf Wildlife Board civil-penalty payment plan owed by Mina Cordero
debts.deadline: Monthly installments continuing through the novel

debts.creditor: Marine engine loan tied to Tomas Acosta's personal skiff and tool debt
debts.status: Active; default risk rises once overtime and county payroll fail.

debts.creditor: Deferred structural repair invoices carried by Rookery Key Bird Camp
debts.status: Active and central to camp decisions.

income_sources.reliability: Weekly at story start; lost in chapter_16, then replaced in chapter_18 by lower but regular restricted-guide wages when bookings hold.

income_sources.source: Tomas's county mosquito-control pay and overtime
income_sources.reliability: Biweekly while active; overtime stops at suspension, and by chapter_18 he is no longer on county payroll.
Monthly and stable short term, but dependent on permits, housing, and grant compliance.

income_sources.source: Rookery Key guest bookings and donor gifts
income_sources.reliability: Irregular and worse after the spray damage and monitoring restrictions.

knowledge_state.knows: She carried a cooler out of BrightCurrent for Jonah Pike and cannot prove the first eel she publicized was wild.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Imogen Price, Tomas Acosta, Beto Alvarez, and initially Rhea Acosta

knowledge_state.knows: He is falsifying county fuel use and route access for Mina.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: County supervisors, Imogen Price, and officially Rhea Acosta

knowledge_state.knows: Mina's BrightCurrent ties are deeper than the public scandal summary and the tunnel under camp links back to that infrastructure.

knowledge_state.character: Mina Cordero, Rhea Acosta, and Tomas Acosta
knowledge_state.knows: They have all clearly seen the eel beneath peak spoonbill activity, but their evidence is partial.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone else until selective disclosure begins

knowledge_state.knows: The Annex B log records east-sluice release, orders staff not to name the luminescent line in the county form, and tallies at least forty-seven losses.
She has enough metadata to narrow the sighting area and chooses not to give Imogen exact coordinates.

knowledge_state.character: Imogen Price, Rhea Acosta, and Tomas Acosta
knowledge_state.knows: The annex evidence destroys any clean miracle narrative and ties the eel line to BrightCurrent's storm-loss release.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Guests, donors, and officials until chapter_16

knowledge_state.character: Investigators and board representatives on site
knowledge_state.knows: Mina's confession, the logbook, the badge, and the need to freeze guest channel access pending review.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: The broader public while the review is active

knowledge_state.character: Mina Cordero, Rhea Acosta, and Tomas Acosta
knowledge_state.knows: The eel line is reproducing in a specific mangrove basin with at least two adults and luminous egg clusters, but Rhea deletes the exact coordinate record.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Investigators, Imogen Price, donors, and the public

knowledge_state.knows: After the early rains, they see another brief blue trace near the restricted walk and notice west-side recorder activity that suggests the line is still moving through adjacent channels; Mina does not report the sighting directly.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Rhea Acosta, Imogen Price, investigators, donors, and guests

environmental_constants: Blackwater reads as mirror-dark until disturbed, then shows tannin swirls and any blue glow with exaggerated force.
The camp sounds unfinished: creaking pilings, screens, generator thrum, leaks into metal, and damp wood groans in early rains.
Mosquito pressure is constant enough to shape clothing, timing, sleep, and temper.
The air usually carries salt, diesel, mud, mangrove rot, wet rope, and kitchen grease or fish.
Spoonbill presence is noisy, dirty, and physical: pink flashes, racket, guano smell, feather dust, chicks, and breeding panic.
The bat lab smells of guano, alcohol wipes, damp netting, labeled bags, and battery heat.
After the spray, guest-facing spaces carry medicinal bleach, laminated warning placards, and escort-only monitoring signage.
Storm fronts and tide shifts can change access and danger within a single scene.
